In this lesson, we're going to quickly talk about assessing the competitive landscape. And really, assessing the competitive landscape is nothing more than understanding what is currently being offered to prospects in your marketplace. What is being offered to them right now in the way of products and services and how is it being presented to them? How is it being positioned to them? And so we need to understand what competitors are offering and presenting to prospects so that we can uniquely position and differentiate what it is that you are offering. Now, I want you to understand why this is critical. When it comes to direct response marketing, there are three key elements that ultimately determine your success. The first element is the list. When I say list, I'm just referring to who is it that you are speaking to? The more targeted the audience for your message, the better your conversion rate, the better your sales are going to be. List is all about making sure that you are talking to prospects that have an interest in what it is that you are saying, that have the problem that you are referring to, that are interested in the solution that you are presenting. The list, the audience is more important than anything else. The wrong audience and it doesn't matter how good your copy is, how good your offer is, how good your marketing is, how great the product is, you're going to struggle. On the flip side, a great list that is targeted to hot prospects, prospects that again have an urgent need, an urgent desire, an urgent want for the solution that you are providing and even a weak offer, even a weak copy, weak marketing message will still allow you to reap success. The number one most important thing is list, but the second most important thing despite what many marketers understand is the offer. We're going to talk about what the offer is in just a second, but the offer is more important than the actual messaging, the actual wording, the actual copy, if you will. The third element, in order of importance, it first and foremost starts with the right list and then it's that you are presenting the right offer, what we're going to talk about in just a second, which is a superior offer to competitors. Then finally after the offer is the copy, is the actual messaging, is what you say. It's critical that you understand what is being offered to the marketplace so that we can create a superior offer so that we can offer prospects something better and so that what we're offering is perceived as different. An offer is really nothing more than what prospects get when they get it, how they get it and what they need to give and do to get it. Your offer is nothing more than the totality, if you will, of everything that you are giving prospects, all the deliverables and when they get those deliverables and how they access those deliverables and what they need to do to get all those things, the pricing and how they pay and so on. That's what your offer is. Now when it comes to offer elements and it comes to assessing the competitive landscape, these are the things that I want you to look at and look for when looking at your top 10 competitors. First and foremost, you want to identify the top 10 competitors in your nature or marketplace that are selling to your target audience. You want to ask what is the product or service positioning? In other words, when looking at the top 10 competitors, you want to look at what it is that they are offering to prospects and how is it being positioned? How is it being presented? Is it being presented, for example, as an e-book, as a coaching program, as a digital download? Is it being presented as a membership site? We want to understand what your top 10 competitors are offering and how they're positioning it to the marketplace so that later on we can position your product or service differently uniquely to the marketplace so you're not just more of the same. You want to ask what are the deliverables? What are your top 10 competitors actually giving to prospects? Are they just providing a PDF? Are they providing videos? Are they providing digitally downloadable mind maps, flow charts? Are they providing CDs, DVDs? Is there any print material? What is it that they are actually delivering? Again, this allows you to understand what is being provided to the marketplace so that we can ensure what you are providing is different. Of course, what is the price point? What are the price points that your top 10 competitors are selling their competitive product or service at? What are the payment terms? Meaning are competitors offering payment plans? Are they offering what's called a is it a hard offer? The prospect has to pay now and then they get the product. Is it a soft offer? Meaning soft payment terms where the prospect can get the product now and pay in 14 days or 21 days or 30 days. Is it a trial where they pay a dollar today and then the remainder in 30 days or 21 days? What are the payment terms? Again, you need to understand these things so that when we're crafting your offer, we're able to craft a superior offer. What are the payment options? Again, this is, are they offering PayPal? Are they offering all the major credit cards? What are the different payment options that they are offering to prospects? What are the premiums and bonuses that they're offering? What types of bonuses are they including with their main product? What is the guarantee? Are they providing any guarantee? Are they providing a 30-day guarantee, a one-year guarantee, a no-questions-ask guarantee? Is it a conditional guarantee? Is it an unconditional guarantee? These are things that we're going to be talking about later on in the six-figure funnel formula, but you need to understand, again, what their guarantee or risk reversal proposition is. By asking all these questions as you're reviewing your top 10 competitors, it gives you an idea of what is currently being presented to prospects. Again, what we present is different, unique, and superior. Your goal, like I've been saying this whole time, when we get to the offer creation portion of the six-figure funnel formula, is that you create a far superior offer to what is currently being offered by competitors. Remember, the offer is the second most important thing, second to just the list. It's our goal to create an offer that is far better and far more irresistible to what is currently being offered to your market.
